sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution satisfying the requirements of City Charter allowing the City to
participate in Alameda County Purchase Order Contract, Master Contract No. 901979
and authorizing the City Manager to execute a purchase order for two (2) 2025
Chevrolet Blazer EV Police Pursuit vehicles with California Automotive Retailing Group
dba Dublin Chevrolet in an amount not to exceed $220,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The purchase of two (2) 2025 Chevrolet Blazer EV Police Pursuit vehicles will not
exceed $220,000 and includes specialized equipment upfitting, CA tire fees, delivery to
City of Berkeley (“City”), and sales tax.
This purchase is on behalf of the Police Department. Funding for this purchase is
available in the 2025 Budget Fund for Equipment Replacement and utilizing funds that
the Police Department has previously paid for vehicle replacement.
sections.current_situation:
The Police Department replaces patrol vehicles on a routine basis. Police Patrol
vehicles require specialized equipment upfitting.
This electric powered vehicle purchase will replace two (2) gas powered existing
vehicles that have exceeded the end of their useful life. The replacement vehicles are
needed by the City’s Police Department Patrol Division in their work to provide public
safety services in Berkeley. The two (2) vehicles identified for replacement are
Equipment #s 1833 and 1822 which are gas Ford Explorers put in service in 2017 and
2016 and are overdue for replacement. The Police Department may identify alternate
vehicles for replacement due to operational needs and with authorization from
Equipment Maintenance.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Authorize Purchase Order with California Automotive Retailing Group dba CONSENT CALENDAR
Dublin Chevrolet for Two 2025 Chevrolet Blazer EV Police Pursuit Vehicles January 21, 2024
Police Patrol vehicles and specialized equipment upfitting will be purchased from
California Automotive Retailing Group dba Dublin Chevrolet utilizing the Alameda
County Purchase Order Contract No. 901979.
Having identified a vendor established through a competitive process will save
considerable staff time that would otherwise be associated with each vehicle purchase.
These replacement vehicles are needed by the City’s Police Department Patrol Division
in their work to provide public safety services in Berkeley.
This purchase is a Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing our goal to create a
resilient, safe, connected and prepared City.
sections.background:
If a purchase request exceeds $100,000.00, the Department of Finance, General
Services Division solicits bids or “piggybacks” off competitively bid contracts to ensure
the City’s departments are receiving the best pricing. On October 1, 2021, California
Automotive Retailing Group dba Dublin Chevrolet, entered into a contract for Police
Patrol vehicles, through a competitive bidding process through the County of Alameda.
On June 16, 2021, the County of Alameda General Services Agency released Request
for Quotation (RFQ) No. 901979 for Vehicle Purchase. RFQ No. 901979 language
required that the award goes to the qualified bidder with the lowest markup over the
triple-net dealer invoice price. Triple-net is the dealer’s actual cost of the vehicles after
all discounts, rebates, and deductions are applied.
On July 27, 2021, multiple bids were received and upon review by the evaluation
committee, Nicholas K Corp dba the Ford Store San Leandro and California Automotive
Retailing Group dba Dublin Chevrolet were determined to be the lowest responsive
responsible bidder and were both awarded County of Alameda Purchase Order
Contract (Master Contract No. 901979 which is extended through September 30, 2026).
The County of Alameda’s bid procedure satisfies the procurement requirements of the
City. This purchase is for Chevrolet vehicles which is why the City need to have a
purchase order with Dublin Chevrolet.
Throughout the year, the Department of Public Works purchases vehicles for the City’s
operating departments. This is paid for through the Equipment Replacement Fund. If a
purchase will exceed $50,000, the Department of Finance, General Services Division
solicits bids or “piggybacks” off competitively bid contracts to ensure the City’s
departments receive the best pricing.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Public Works continues to examine ways to support the City’s climate action goals
through the procurement the most fuel-efficient vehicles and equipment th
sections.rationale:
Gas powered Police Patrol vehicles have been overdue for replacement. A purchase
order is needed to secure these vehicles for the City. Piggybacking on pricing obtained
through another public agency’s competitive bidding process ensures that the City is
receiving the most competitive pricing.
sections.alternatives:
The purchase of gas-powered hybrid Police Patrol vehicles was considered. That
purchase action was rejected as it would not advance the state mandate that requires
government fleets to be zero-emission vehicles by 2035, and this purchase allows the
Police Department to fully evaluate the performance of an all-electric vehicle.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Don Colbert, Acting Equipment Superintendent, (510) 981-6467
Sara Rubio, Associate Management Analyst, (510) 981-5372
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PURCHASE ORDER: CALIFORNIA AUTOMOTIVE RETAILING GROUP DBA DUBLIN
CHEVROLET FOR TWO CHEVROLET BLAZER EV POLICE PURSUIT VEHICLES
WHEREAS, vehicles are needed by the City Police Department in their work to provide
public safety services in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, two (2) gas powered Police Patrol vehicles have reached the end of their
useful life and replacements are needed to meet public safety and operational
requirements; and
WHEREAS, Alameda County Purchase Order Contract No.901979 contract bid
procedures satisfy the procurement requirements of the City of Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, funds will be allocated from FY 2025 baseline Budget Fund of Equipment
Replacement, Fund 671.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a purchase order for 2025 Chevrolet Blazer EV
Police Pursuit vehicles in the amount not to exceed $220,000.
